steps.title: Identify what follows 'likely'
steps.content: After “were more likely,” the sentence structure is: [verb form] + [noun object]. The blank requires a verb form that will complete this pattern.

steps.title: Apply the infinitive rule
steps.content: After an adjective meaning likelihood, Standard English requires an infinitive (to + base verb). “To eat” correctly provides the infinitive; eaten, ate, and will be eating violate this by using a past participle, simple past, and future progressive instead. That matches choice B — B is correct.
